summary: Comprehensive mapping of PGlite and DuckDB initialization patterns across the codebase

pglite_initialization.summary: PGlite (@electric-sql/pglite) is the embedded Postgres engine for desktop/local deployment. It runs in-process via WASM, requiring no Docker or system Postgres.

init_function.signature: init(connectionStringOrOpts: string | InitOptions, ssl?: boolean): PgClient

behavior.detection: Lines 27: Checks if connectionString is ':memory:', starts with '/', or starts with 'file:'
behavior.pglite_path: Line 31: new PGlite(dataDir) — PGlite constructor called with optional dataDir (undefined for :memory:)
behavior.drizzle_pglite: Line 32: drizzle({ client }) as PgliteDatabase — wraps in Drizzle ORM
behavior.node_postgres_path: Lines 34-45: Falls through to new Pool({...}) for production Postgres connections

direct_init_in_db_ts.behavior: Legacy global singleton pattern. Client = ReturnType<typeof init>. Lazily inits on first call via Object.assign function with .reset/.loaded methods.
direct_init_in_db_ts.path_detection: Line 33-36: Flag.OPENCODE_DB can be ':memory:', absolute path, or relative name (joined to Global.Path.data)

migration_entry.behavior: runMigrations() uses detectDialect() which checks OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL env var

applyMigrations.pglite_path: Lines 57-65: Duck-type checks if $client.exec() exists. If yes, reads migration files manually via readMigrationFiles() and iterates SQL statements
applyMigrations.node_postgres_path: Lines 67-68: Uses drizzle-orm's built-in migrate() function for node-postgres
applyMigrations.error_handling: None — no try/catch around migration execution. Failure propagates as unhandled rejection.

runMigrations.sqlite_path: Lines 34-42: Bun SQLite database + drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite migrator
runMigrations.pg_path: Lines 44-59: Calls initPg(url, ssl) then applyMigrations(client). Closes underlying pool/client in finally block.
runMigrations.cleanup: Line 57: Duck-type checks for $client.end() — PGlite has no end(), node-postgres Pool does
runMigrations.error_handling: Line 48: Throws Error if url is missing for PG path. Lines 60-66: CLI catch handler logs and exits 1.

error_handling.pglite_init: NO try/catch around new PGlite(dataDir). If PGlite fails to construct (WASM load failure, disk error), the error propagates as an unhandled rejection.
error_handling.pool_init: NO try/catch around new Pool(...) either. Both paths fail hard on initialization failure.
error_handling.adapter_layer: File: adapter.ts lines 183-188: makePgAdapter calls initPg(options) without try/catch at the construction site. Errors caught downstream by Effect.tryPromise wrapper (line 196).

fallback_behavior.sqlite_fallback: File: adapter.ts line 375-376: If config.url is None (no OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL), falls back to SQLiteAdapter — uses legacy Database from db.ts which uses Bun SQLite or PGlite via #db import.
fallback_behavior.pglite_init_failure: NO retry or fallback — PGlite init failure at the direct Database.Client() path (db.ts) is fatal. The adapter layer wraps in Effect which catches the error, but doesn't retry.
fallback_behavior.migration_failure: migrate.ts CLI entry: catches and exits 1. No automatic retry.

duckdb_initialization.summary: DuckDB is an analytical sidecar — separate from the transactional database. It has 4+ independent initialization paths, all subprocess-based.

cli_client_with_pool.behavior: Creates a DuckDBConnectionPool that manages max 4 concurrent duckdb CLI subprocesses. Each query spawns 'duckdb [path] -readonly -json -c [sql]'.
cli_client_with_pool.readonly_guard: Line 53: -readonly flag on subprocess args prevents writes at the OS level
cli_client_with_pool.exec_timeout: Line 68: 30s execution timeout kills process and rejects

wasm_client_future.behavior: Returns null — deferred. WASM path is placeholder until end-to-end verified.
wasm_client_future.comment: Line 226: 'Return null to use CLI fallback until the WASM integration is verified end-to-end.'

pipeline_subprocess.behavior: Spawns duckdb [path] -json -c [sql] — NO -readonly flag. Pipeline needs write access to create tables.
pipeline_subprocess.timeout: Line 123: 60s timeout kills process and rejects
pipeline_subprocess.note: Bypasses DuckDB.Service completely — firewall is NOT applied here (pipeline is internal only, per line 110 comment)

write_pipe.behavior: Spawns duckdb [path] with SQL piped to stdin. NO -readonly, NO -json, NO -c flag. Full write access.
write_pipe.status: ORPHANED — imported zero times in the tool directory. Was designed for dual-backend writes that were never implemented.

rig_jsonl_query.behavior: Direct execFileAsync — no pool, no firewall, no read-only flag, no connection management.
rig_jsonl_query.note: A fourth independent subprocess path with different security properties.

effect_service_layer.declaration: export const layer: Layer.Layer<Service, never, DuckDBConfig.Service | DatabaseAdapter.Service>
effect_service_layer.behavior: Creates CLI client synchronously, then forks pipeline in background if dbPath is set
effect_service_layer.pipeline_condition: Line 257: Only runs pipeline if dbPath is not undefined (i.e., not :memory:)
effect_service_layer.error_handling: Pipeline errors are ignored via Effect.ignore. Client creation (createCLIClient) is synchronous and never fails — subprocess errors happen at query time.

steps: Line 68: metaCheck — queries _pipeline_meta table, skips if schema_version >= 2
Line 74: Exports session data from Postgres via adapter.query
Line 82: Exports part data from Postgres via adapter.query
Line 91: Exports runtime_events from Postgres via adapter.query (catches DatabaseError gracefully - line 104)
Line 114: Creates _pipeline_session table from JSON via stdin
Line 115: Creates _pipeline_part table from JSON via stdin
Lines 116-118: Creates _pipeline_runtime_event table if events exist
Line 121: Creates analytical tables via initTablesSql()
Line 124: Creates analytical views via initViewsSql()
Line 127: Writes meta as LAST step — if anything above fails, meta not written (idempotency)
Line 130: Overall pipeline failure is caught and logged as non-fatal (Effect.catch → logError)

error_handling.subprocess_spawn_fail: File: db.duckdb.ts line 106: 'Failed to spawn duckdb' → DuckDBError. The error propagates through the Effect layer.
error_handling.caller_handling: File: tool/duckdb-query.ts line 44: Checks error message for 'Failed to spawn duckdb' and returns a helpful user-facing message 'DuckDB binary is not installed or not found in PATH.' with install instructions.
error_handling.firewall_errors: File: duckdb-firewall.ts line 45: DuckDBFirewallError thrown for blocked functions (read_text, ATTACH, LOAD, etc.)
error_handling.pipeline_errors: File: pipeline.ts line 130: Non-fatal — pipeline failure is caught and logged, app continues without DuckDB analytics.

graceful_degradation.duckdb_missing: File: tool/duckdb-query.ts line 44-55: Detects spawn failure, returns user-friendly error message. Tool remains registered but returns an error when called. No automatic fallback.
graceful_degradation.experimental_flag: File: effect/runtime-flags.ts line 54: experimentalDuckDBQuery is gated behind OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_DUCKDB_QUERY env var. File: tool/registry.ts line 476: duckdb_query tool is excluded from tool list when flag is false.
graceful_degradation.pipeline_failure: File: pipeline.ts line 130: Non-fatal, app continues without analytical views
graceful_degradation.no_automatic_fallback: There is NO automatic fallback to a non-DuckDB analytics path. If DuckDB fails, analytical features (failure hotspots, slow tools, file contention, etc.) are unavailable.

storage_abstraction.summary: Two separate storage abstractions exist: DatabaseAdapter (transactional) and Storage (filesystem-based). DuckDB is a third path outside both.

implementations.SQLiteAdapter: Line 86: makeSQLiteAdapter() — wraps legacy Database singleton with DrizzleLikeClient bridge. For post-Phase 1.7a migration, wraps PGlite raw .exec()/.query().
implementations.PgAdapter: Line 183: makePgAdapter(options) — calls initPg(options) from #db. Has retry logic for serialization failures (40001). Uses sanitizePgError for error redaction.
implementations.DuckDBAdapter: Line 283: makeDuckDBAdapter(client) — read-only. Drizzle select/insert/update/delete/transaction all throw errors. Only all/get/run work.

database_adapter.mode_decision: Line 371: Layer.effect reads DatabaseConfig. If config.url is Some → PgAdapter (Postgres via PGlite or node-postgres). Else → SQLiteAdapter.

retry_behavior.serialization_retry: Line 249: Retries serialization failures (code '40001') up to 3 times with exponential backoff
retry_behavior.sqlite_retry: Line 137: Transaction retry for SQLite (3 times, exponential)
retry_behavior.pg_retry: Line 240: Transaction uses Effect.retry with while filter for DatabaseError.isRetryable

legacy_database.note: '@deprecated use DatabaseAdapter.Service instead' in node.ts line 5
legacy_database.behavior: Global singleton pattern. Calls init from #db (db.pg.ts) with path from getPath(). Returns Drizzle ORM client directly.
legacy_database.path_logic: Lines 33-35: Flag.OPENCODE_DB → can be ':memory:' or absolute path. Otherwise getChannelPath() → 'opencode.db' or 'opencode-{channel}.db'
legacy_database.migration_skipping: Line 20: RuntimeFlags service includes skipMigrations flag

duckdb_storage_relationship.separate_infrastructure: DuckDB does NOT route through DatabaseAdapter. It has its own service tag (@opencode/DuckDB), its own connection pool, its own CLI spawn pattern, its own config service, its own firewall, its own error types.
duckdb_storage_relationship.adapter_bridge_exists: makeDuckDBAdapter() in adapter.ts wraps DuckDBRawClient into the DrizzleLikeClient interface, but this is NOT used in the DuckDB layer itself — it's an adapter FOR other code that wants to use DatabaseAdapter interface with DuckDB underneath.

known_orphans.makeDuckDBAdapter: File: adapter.ts line 283-350. Defined but references to it in adapter.ts line 355 show it's used by DuckDBAdapter Layer, but this Layer is not wired into the default layer pipeline.
known_orphans.execDuckDBWrite: File: duckdb-pipe.ts line 23. Exported but imported zero times.

storage_service.description: Filesystem-based storage abstraction — not a database. Uses AppFileSystem and Git for file operations. Supports remove/read/update/write/list. Separate from both PGlite and DuckDB.

retry_logic.pg_transaction_serialization: adapter.ts line 247: Retries 3 times, exponential backoff 100ms, only for error code '40001'
retry_logic.sqlite_transaction: adapter.ts line 137: Retries 3 times, exponential backoff 100ms, all errors
retry_logic.pg_pool_connection: pg-pool.ts line 132: Retries up to maxRetries times with exponential backoff, only for retryable errors (DNS/network/timeout vs auth/SSL)
retry_logic.duckdb_retry: NONE — DuckDB queries are fire-and-forget. No retry logic.

fallback_gaps.pglite_init_failure: No fallback. If PGlite WASM fails to load, the app crashes.
fallback_gaps.pglite_migration_failure: No fallback. Migration failure is fatal in CLI mode (exit 1).
fallback_gaps.duckdb_missing_binary: User-friendly error message but no automatic degradation — DuckDB-dependent features are simply unavailable.
fallback_gaps.duckdb_pipeline_failure: Non-fatal. Pipeline failures are caught and logged, app continues without analytical views.
fallback_gaps.experimental_gating: DuckDB query tool is gated behind OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_DUCKDB_QUERY flag (off by default).

direct_imports: storage/adapter.ts — imports init as initPg from #db (line 4)
storage/migrate.ts — imports init and applyMigrations from #db (line 4)
depends_on_pglite_postgres.scopes: All transactional data: sessions, messages, parts, todos, permissions, accounts, projects, workspaces, shares, runtime events

depends_on_duckdb.duckdb_query_tool: tool/duckdb-query.ts — requires 'duckdb' CLI binary in PATH
depends_on_duckdb.rig_jsonl_query_tool: tool/rig-jsonl-query.ts — requires 'duckdb' CLI binary in PATH
depends_on_duckdb.event_queries: event/event-queries.ts — typed query functions for DuckDB analytical views. Takes DuckDBRawClient as parameter.
depends_on_duckdb.debug_export: debug/debug-export.ts — includes 'duckdb-queries.json' in debug packet export (line 110)
depends_on_duckdb.pipeline: storage/pipeline.ts — data pipeline that populates DuckDB analytical tables from Postgres data

depends_on_neither.storage_service: storage/storage.ts — file-based storage abstraction (AppFileSystem + Git). Independent.

notable_gaps.gap: PGlite init has no error handling
notable_gaps.detail: new PGlite(dataDir) is called without try/catch. WASM initialization failure crashes the process.

notable_gaps.gap: No PGlite fallback to SQLite on init failure
notable_gaps.detail: If PGlite fails during init, there is no automatic fallback to SQLite. The adapter layer's decision to use PgAdapter vs SQLiteAdapter is based on config (OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL presence), not on runtime availability.

notable_gaps.gap: DuckDB has 4+ independent spawn paths with different security profiles
files: rig-jsonl-query.ts (execFileAsync, -json only, NO firewall, NO pool)
notable_gaps.detail: Each path has different security properties. Only the primary path (db.duckdb.ts) enforces -readonly and firewall. The others are unrestricted.

notable_gaps.gap: DuckDBAdapter Layer is not wired into the default Layer pipeline
notable_gaps.detail: DuckDBAdapter is exported but its layer is not composed into the default adapter layer. It appears to be unused infrastructure.
execDuckDBWrite is never imported. The dual-backend write pattern was planned but never implemented.
DataMigrationTable uses drizzle-orm/sqlite-core. Data migrations only work on SQLite path. Would fail with 'table not found' on PostgreSQL.

notable_gaps.gap: Pipeline DB adapter query uses hardcoded Postgres column references
notable_gaps.detail: The pipeline queries Postgres columns like 'CAST(model AS text)' which will fail against the SQLite adapter because SQLite adapter wraps PGlite raw .exec()/.query(), not full Drizzle ORM query building.

notable_gaps.gap: DuckDB schema_version is not checked with -readonly flag
notable_gaps.detail: metaCheck() spawns duckdb without -readonly flag, unlike the main client path. Minimal risk since it only queries _pipeline_meta, but inconsistent.
